articles.title: Senators Seek Answers From Trump Administration About Airport Immigration Arrests
articles.summary: Airport security officials have been sharing passenger data with immigration agents, but the program received little attention until videos captured a woman and her daughter being detained at San Fran

articles.title: Mexico Pressures U.S. Over Deaths of Its Citizens in ICE Custody
articles.summary: Claudia Sheinbaum, president of Mexico, said her country would take legal steps to demand better conditions at immigration detention facilities, where she said 14 Mexican citizens had died since Presi

articles.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
articles.summary: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

articles.title: Army reviewing after helicopters hovered alongside Kid Rock's swimming pool as he saluted
articles.summary: The Army has launched an administrative review after two AH-64 Apache helicopters on a training run hovered near the hillside home of Kid Rock as the outspoken supporter of President Donald Trump salu

articles.title: Airport cleared to be renamed for Trump as he unveils design for skyscraper library
articles.summary: A Florida airport was cleared to be renamed after President Donald Trump on Monday, hours before the president revealed plans for a Miami skyscraper planned to house his presidential library.

articles.title: Women's Final Four is set, and it is a repeat of last season's
articles.summary: No. 1 seeds UConn, UCLA, Texas and South Carolina are in the Final Four for the second straight season, just the second time the same teams have reached the sport's final weekend in consecutive years.

articles.title: Thieves steal paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse from a private museum in Italy
articles.summary: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.

articles.title: After Months of Threats, Trump Softens His Stance on Blocking Oil to Cuba
articles.summary: The White House press secretary said the United States would evaluate oil shipments to Cuba on a “case-by-case basis,” after a Russian tanker full of crude reached the island.

articles.title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
articles.summary: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.

articles.title: A New U.S. Missile Hit a Sports Hall in Iran. Here’s What We Know About It.
articles.summary: Barely out of prototype testing, the Precision Strike Missile is shrouded in secrecy — including which Persian Gulf countries the Army is launching them from.

articles.title: TSA Workers Begin to Receive Paychecks After Trump Signs Executive Order
articles.summary: A memo that President Trump signed on Friday ordering the Department of Homeland Security to pay T.S.A. officers did not specify whether they would be paid on a regular schedule.

articles.title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
articles.summary: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.

articles.title: Yemeni politician says former U.S. soldiers tried to kill him. Now he's suing in U.S. court
articles.summary: The suit is centered around the alleged attempt on Anssaf Ali Mayo's life. But it raises broader questions, including about the role of the United Arab Emirates in Yemen's civil war.

articles.title: Trump rolls back pause on asylum decisions imposed after D.C. National Guard shooting
articles.summary: The Homeland Security Department has lifted its total ban on reviewing asylum applications, a pause that affected millions of cases. The pause remains in effect for about 40 countries.

articles.title: U.S. could exempt oil industry from protecting Gulf animals, for 'national security'
articles.summary: National security has never been used to call a meeting of the "God Squad." But other federal agencies have been citing the "energy emergency" to avoid rules meant to protect endangered animals.

articles.title: Senate Republicans Make No Move to End DHS Shutdown in Brief Session
articles.summary: With Congress in recess, much of the Homeland Security Department remains without money because of the impasse on Capitol Hill, even as airport security workers are to begin getting paid.

articles.title: Figure skating season ends with redemption and heartbreak. What do fans watch next?
articles.summary: Worlds marks the last competition of the 2025-2026 season. Skaters have some time to go on tour, rest up and learn new routines before the next season starts in July.

articles.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
articles.summary: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

articles.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.

articles.title: A skeleton discovered in a Dutch church may belong to musketeer d'Artagnan
articles.summary: Human remains found in a church in the Netherlands could be those of d'Artagnan, one of the legendary French swordsmen who inspired the novel The Three Musketeers.

articles.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
articles.summary: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

articles.title: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
articles.summary: Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.

articles.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
articles.summary: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
